pattern: Since only the set of letters in a word matters, each word collapses to a 26-bit fingerprint, and 'connected' becomes a graph problem over those fingerprints that a union-find can answer without ever comparing words to each other directly.
hints: Because no word repeats a letter, every word can be reduced to a bitmask of which of the 26 letters it contains -- from that point on you never need the original word again, only its mask.

For each mask, only a small, fixed number of other masks can ever be directly reachable from it: masks with exactly one extra bit set, masks with exactly one bit cleared, and masks the same size that differ by one swapped bit -- so build a union-find and merge a mask with each of those neighbors that actually occurs among the words.

The tricky neighbor type is the swapped-bit case: instead of checking all 26x26 possible swaps, merge each mask with a shared 'one bit removed' key -- two masks of the same size that reduce to the exact same key after removing one bit are exactly the ones connected by a single swap, so they end up in the same union-find group automatically; count words (not masks) per group root to get the group count and the largest size.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, two connected pairs plus one isolated word
statement example, a chain of three words all in one group
the exact same word repeated several times, all one group
anagrams of one another share an identical letter set
same-length words with completely disjoint letters stay in separate groups
single-letter words of different letters are all mutually swap-connected into one group
a growing chain where each word adds one new letter to the last
two words that together use every letter of the alphabet with no overlap

stress.description: near the maximum word count, each word a random subset of letters in random order, exercising the full bitmask union-find at scale
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 20000))
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        length = rng.randint(1, 26)
        letters = rng.sample(range(26), length)
        words.append(''.join(chr(97 + x) for x in letters))
    return [words]
stress.note: letters within each word are sampled without replacement so no word ever repeats a letter, matching the constraint
